name: Ecuador
avoid: October–November can bring heavier rain, cloudy mountain views, and muddier road conditions around Mindo and Baños.
Carnaval, Semana Santa, and July–August school holidays push up Galápagos flights, island rooms, and last-minute day-tour prices quickly.
Quito and Cuenca both sit high enough for altitude fatigue, so keep your first day light before packing in church circuits or long bus rides.
stopoverNote: Quito is the easiest international gateway and the most practical buffer city before Galápagos flights, because most island departures still connect through Quito or Guayaquil.

cities.name: Quito
cities.notes: High-altitude capital for colonial churches, TelefériQo views, museums, and the easiest jump-off point for both Otavalo day loops and Galápagos flights.

cities.name: Otavalo
cities.notes: Imbabura market town known for Plaza de Ponchos textiles, nearby Peguche waterfall, and easy access to Cuicocha crater viewpoints.

cities.name: Mindo
cities.notes: Cloud-forest escape for butterfly gardens, hummingbirds, chocolate stops, river tubing, and waterfall hikes without going far from Quito.

cities.name: Baños
cities.notes: Adventure base below Tungurahua for Ruta de las Cascadas, Pailón del Diablo, hot springs, canyoning, and the famous Casa del Árbol swing.

cities.name: Cuenca
cities.notes: UNESCO-listed Andean city of blue-domed cathedrals, riverside walks, Panama-hat workshops, and easy side trips to Ingapirca.

cities.name: Galápagos Islands
cities.notes: Wildlife-heavy island circuit usually based from Puerto Ayora or San Cristóbal, with giant tortoises, lava scenery, snorkelling, and inter-island lanchas.

itinerary.sim: Claro, Movistar, or Tuenti prepago/eSIM usually runs about ₹840–₹1,260 for 10–15GB. Coverage is solid in Quito and Cuenca, but it gets patchier in Mindo, on Baños canyon roads, and on outer Galápagos boat legs, so download offline maps before long transfers.

Quito.name: Quito
Quito.note: Stay around Centro Histórico, La Floresta, or La Mariscal, keep arrival day light because Quito sits above 2,800 m, and use Uber or inDrive after dark instead of flagging random taxis.

Basilica del Voto Nacional nave and tower access
Iglesia de la Compañía de Jesús interior

Capilla del Hombre
Museo Casa del Alabado
Itchimbía or Parque Metropolitano sunset viewpoint

activities.name: Ciudad Mitad del Mundo monument complex
Museo Intiñan
Museo Templo del Sol Pintor Ortega Maila

days.theme: Free Day for Papallacta, Museums or Flight Buffer

activities.name: Keep this day as a weather or altitude buffer before a long bus ride or Galápagos departure
Optional Papallacta hot springs day trip
Mercado Artesanal La Mariscal souvenirs and coffee

Otavalo.name: Otavalo
Otavalo.note: Saturday is the strongest market day, but weekday visits are still worthwhile if you pair Plaza de Ponchos with Peguche or Cuicocha.

activities.name: Browse Plaza de Ponchos textile market and nearby leather and jewelry lanes
Taxi or short bus hop to Cascada de Peguche
Condor Park demonstration and rescue center
Light evening around Parque Simón Bolívar and artisan cafés
Bus or taxi to Laguna Cuicocha rim viewpoints
Cuicocha boat ride when operating
Peguche weaving workshop stop on the return

Mindo.name: Mindo
Mindo.note: Afternoon rain is common even in the better months, so do birding or waterfall loops early and keep a dry bag ready for tubing or tarabita rides.

activities.name: Mariposario de Mindo butterfly house
Tarabita and Sendero a las Cascadas de Nambillo circuit
El Quetzal chocolate tour
Early hummingbird session at Sachatamia Lodge or another cloud-forest reserve
Mindo river tubing
Orquideario de Mindo and coffee stop

Baños.name: Baños
Baños.note: Use Baños for active days rather than luxury downtime; the town is compact, but waterfalls and adventure operators spread along the road toward Puyo.

days.theme: Ruta de las Cascadas & Hot Springs

activities.name: Bike rental or chiva along the Ruta de las Cascadas
Pailón del Diablo entrance
Manto de la Novia tarabita
Termas de la Virgen night soak
Casa del Árbol entry and Swing at the End of the World
Basilica of Our Lady of the Holy Water and central plaza
Rafting or canyoning half-day
Optional zipline or bridge-jump combo

days.theme: Slow Morning or Chimborazo Transfer Buffer

activities.name: Keep this day for recovery, café-hopping, or a simple Virgen waterfall viewpoint walk before the Cuenca transfer
Optional massage or thermal spa treatment

Cuenca.name: Cuenca
Cuenca.note: Cuenca feels calmer than Quito, so it works well after Baños; stay near Parque Calderón or the Tomebamba river so churches, museums, and dining are all walkable.

activities.name: Catedral de la Inmaculada Concepción dome or museum access
Old Cathedral Museum (Iglesia del Sagrario)
Museo Pumapungo and archaeological zone
Río Tomebamba walk and flower market around Plaza de las Flores
Bus or shared transfer from Cuenca toward Ingapirca
Ingapirca archaeological complex entry
Homero Ortega Panama hat factory or showroom stop on return

days.theme: Cajas National Park or Extra Cuenca Day

activities.name: Shared trip to Cajas National Park lagoons and trails
Museum of Modern Art or artisan quarter browsing back in town

Galápagos Islands.name: Galápagos Islands
Galápagos Islands.note: Base in Puerto Ayora if you want the widest hotel and food choice, carry cash in USD for park and pier fees, and expect rougher water on fast local lanchas between islands.

days.theme: Flight to Baltra, Park Fee & Tortuga Bay

activities.name: LATAM flight from Quito or Guayaquil to Baltra
Galápagos Transit Control Card
Galápagos National Park entry
Lobito bus, Itabaca Channel ferry, and taxi or bus into Puerto Ayora
Tortuga Bay beach walk and sunset
Charles Darwin Research Station and giant tortoise breeding center
Las Grietas water taxi and access
El Chato Ranch giant tortoise reserve
Playa de los Alemanes or Garrapatero beach if you want a slower afternoon
Fast lancha between Santa Cruz and Isabela or San Cristóbal
Tintoreras islet snorkel from Isabela or a simple San Cristóbal bay walk
Small pier or water-taxi fees

days.theme: Extra Boat Tour or Snorkel Buffer

activities.name: North Seymour, Bartolomé, or similar full-day boat excursion
Mask, fins, or wetsuit rental top-up
Keep this day flexible because marine conditions and passenger checks can reshuffle island departures

connections.method: Cooperativa Otavalo or similar frequent bus from Terminal Carcelén, roughly 2–2.5 hrs depending on traffic
Usually a two-leg route via Quito using an Otavalo cooperative bus back south and Flor del Valle onward to Mindo; private transfer is faster but much pricier
Bus back via Quito or Santo Domingo and onward through Ambato; long but workable if you leave early
Cooperative bus via Ambato or Riobamba, typically 6–7 hrs including one terminal change
LATAM flight via Quito or Guayaquil into Baltra; build in buffer time because same-day island check-in rules are strict

extras: June–September is the cleanest highland weather window for Quito, Otavalo, Cuenca, and Baños, while December–May is warmer and greener for the coast and Galápagos sea life.
Carry some USD cash for the Galápagos park fee, transit control card, water taxis, and smaller island restaurants because cards do not work everywhere.
Quito and Cuenca both sit at altitude, so avoid stacking your heaviest church-and-stairs day or a same-night overnight bus immediately after landing.
Otavalo market is strongest on Saturdays, but even weekday visits are worth it if you pair them with Peguche or Cuicocha.
Fast lanchas between islands can be rough, so pack motion-sickness tablets and keep valuables in a waterproof pouch.

Baños: Basilica of Our Lady of the Holy Water Banos
